title: Viewport rendering: normals fallback, double-sided materials, data validation, buffer updates
analysis:
This task fixes 8 viewport rendering issues across 3 source files (meshManager.ts, selection.ts, scene.ts) with coordination in Viewport.tsx. The codebase uses a factory-function pattern (createScene, createMeshManager, createSelection) with Three.js fully mocked via vi.mock in vitest tests (jsdom environment).

Current bugs and fixes:
1. V-04 (meshManager.ts:53-55): When data.normals is null, no computeVertexNormals() fallback — causes flat-black faces. Fix: call geometry.computeVertexNormals() when normals are null in both createMeshFromData and updateMeshGeometry.
2. V-05 (meshManager.ts:57-59): MeshStandardMaterial lacks DoubleSide — invisible back-faces on thin CAD shells. Fix: add side: THREE.DoubleSide to material constructor.
3. V-06 (meshManager.ts): No validation of mesh data before BufferGeometry creation. Fix: validate vertices.length % 3 === 0 and indices in bounds before creating/updating geometry.
4. V-07 (meshManager.ts:66-105): updateMeshGeometry mutates BufferAttribute internals (array + count) unsafely — when array length changes, the WebGL buffer size is fixed, causing errors. Fix: create new BufferAttribute when array length differs.
5. V-08 (selection.ts:139-153): Selection wireframe not synced after geometry updates. Fix: add refreshSelected() to SelectionContext and call it from Viewport.tsx after mesh sync.
6. V-11 (scene.ts:34): Fixed camera clipping planes (0.1, 10000). Fix: add adjustClipping(sceneBounds) method to SceneContext that computes near/far from camera-to-scene distance.
7. V-13 (scene.ts:44-49): Single directional light with no camera-following headlight. Fix: add a headlight as a child of the camera.
8. V-15 (scene.ts:58-62): setPixelRatio not updated on display change/resize. Fix: call renderer.setPixelRatio(window.devicePixelRatio) in resize handler.

The existing test infrastructure provides extensive mock classes. The MockBufferGeometry needs computeVertexNormals added. The three mock in meshManager.test.ts needs DoubleSide and FrontSide constants. Scene tests need MockPerspectiveCamera.add method. The existing Viewport.tsx effect pattern (createEffect depending on props.meshes) provides the coordination point for calling refreshSelected and adjustClipping after mesh sync.

steps.description: Write failing test in meshManager.test.ts: when data.normals is null, createMeshFromData calls geometry.computeVertexNormals() (V-04). Add computeVertexNormals mock to MockBufferGeometry. Test: sync with null normals, assert computeVertexNormals was called. Also test: sync with provided normals, assert computeVertexNormals was NOT called.

Implement V-04 in meshManager.ts: In createMeshFromData, add else branch after the normals check — when data.normals is null, call geometry.computeVertexNormals(). In updateMeshGeometry, in the else-if branch where normals are removed (line 97-98), call geometry.computeVertexNormals() after deleteAttribute.
Write failing test in meshManager.test.ts: material is created with side: THREE.DoubleSide (V-05). Add DoubleSide constant to mock. Assert that the material's side property equals DoubleSide (2).
Implement V-05 in meshManager.ts: Import DoubleSide from 'three'. Add side: DoubleSide to the MeshStandardMaterial constructor options.

Write failing tests in meshManager.test.ts for mesh data validation (V-06). Tests: (1) sync with vertices.length not divisible by 3 does not add mesh to scene, (2) sync with an index >= vertex count does not add mesh, (3) valid data still works normally. Also test update path: update with invalid data skips the update.

Implement V-06 in meshManager.ts: Add validateMeshData(data: MeshData): boolean that checks vertices.length % 3 === 0 and max(indices) < vertices.length / 3. Guard createMeshFromData and updateMeshGeometry calls in sync() with this validation, logging a console.warn for invalid data.

Write failing tests in meshManager.test.ts for safe buffer updates when array length changes (V-07). Tests: (1) update with same-length arrays reuses existing BufferAttribute (identity check — existing test), (2) update with different-length vertex array creates a new BufferAttribute (not same reference), (3) update with different-length index array creates new index BufferAttribute, (4) new BufferAttribute has correct array and count.

Implement V-07 in meshManager.ts: In updateMeshGeometry, before reusing posAttr, check if posAttr.array.length !== data.vertices.length — if so, create new BufferAttribute via setAttribute instead of mutating. Same logic for index and normals.

Write failing test in scene.test.ts: resize handler calls renderer.setPixelRatio with window.devicePixelRatio (V-15). Call resize(), assert mockSetPixelRatio was called. Verify it uses the current devicePixelRatio value.
Implement V-15 in scene.ts: In the resize() function, add renderer.setPixelRatio(window.devicePixelRatio ?? 1) before renderer.setSize().

Write failing test in scene.test.ts: scene has a camera-following headlight (V-13). Add camera.add mock to MockPerspectiveCamera. Assert that a DirectionalLight is added as a child of the camera (via camera.add), and that the camera itself is added to the scene (scene.add(camera)).

Implement V-13 in scene.ts: Create a second DirectionalLight (headlight) at position (0, 0, 1) relative to camera with intensity ~0.6. Call camera.add(headlight) to make it follow the camera. Call scene.add(camera) so camera children are rendered in the scene graph.

Write failing test in scene.test.ts: SceneContext exposes adjustClipping method (V-11). Test that adjustClipping(bounds) updates camera.near and camera.far based on scene size and that updateProjectionMatrix is called.

Implement V-11 in scene.ts: Add adjustClipping(sceneBounds: Box3) to SceneContext interface. Implementation computes camera-to-center distance, sets near = max(distance * 0.001, 0.01) and far = max(distance * 10, 100), calls camera.updateProjectionMatrix(). Export from createScene return. Update Viewport.tsx to call adjustClipping after mesh sync.

Write failing test in selection.test.ts: SelectionContext exposes refreshSelected() that rebuilds wireframe for current selection (V-08). Tests: (1) refreshSelected with active selection removes old wireframe and creates new one, (2) refreshSelected with no selection is a no-op, (3) refreshSelected reflects updated geometry.

Implement V-08 in selection.ts: Track currentSelectedPath in a closure variable. Add refreshSelected() method that calls setSelected(currentSelectedPath) to rebuild wireframe from current geometry. Export in SelectionContext. Update Viewport.tsx to call selection.refreshSelected() after meshManager.sync() in the mesh sync effect.

design_decisions.decision: Camera headlight is added as a child of the camera object via camera.add() rather than updating position each frame
design_decisions.rationale: Three.js automatically transforms child objects in the parent's local space, so a light added to camera follows it without manual position updates in the render loop. This is the standard Three.js pattern for headlights and avoids polluting the animation loop.

design_decisions.decision: Dynamic clipping is exposed as an adjustClipping(sceneBounds: Box3) method called after mesh sync, not computed every frame
design_decisions.rationale: Computing scene bounds every frame via Box3 traversal is expensive. Clipping only needs adjustment when mesh data changes (during sync), which is the only time bounds change. This keeps the render loop lightweight.

design_decisions.decision: Mesh data validation logs console.warn and skips invalid data rather than throwing
design_decisions.rationale: The viewport should be resilient to bad data from the engine. Throwing would crash the entire render pipeline. A console.warn is sufficient for debugging while keeping the UI responsive. This follows the existing project preference for noisy errors on logic bugs (feedback_silent_defaults_pattern.md) while still being resilient.

design_decisions.decision: New BufferAttribute is created when array length changes; existing attribute is mutated only when length matches
design_decisions.rationale: WebGL buffers have a fixed size allocated on the GPU. Mutating .array and .count on an existing BufferAttribute when the size differs causes WebGL errors because the underlying GPU buffer cannot be resized. When sizes match, reusing the attribute avoids GPU buffer reallocation overhead — this is the intended Three.js pattern.

design_decisions.decision: refreshSelected() method added to SelectionContext rather than coupling meshManager directly to selection
design_decisions.rationale: Keeps modules decoupled. Viewport.tsx already coordinates between meshManager and selection via reactive effects. Adding a refresh method is the minimal change that preserves this clean architecture. refreshSelected internally just calls setSelected(currentSelectedPath).

design_decisions.decision: computeVertexNormals() is called in both createMeshFromData and updateMeshGeometry when normals are null
design_decisions.rationale: Both code paths can receive null normals. The fallback must be applied consistently in creation and update paths to prevent flat-black faces in any scenario.

design_decisions.decision: scene.add(camera) is called to support camera children (headlight)
design_decisions.rationale: Three.js requires parent objects to be in the scene graph for their children to be rendered. Without scene.add(camera), the headlight attached via camera.add() would not be visible. The camera itself renders fine without being in the scene, but its children do not.

reuse.what: Existing vi.mock('three') patterns and MockXxx classes in all viewport test files
reuse.how: Extend existing mock blocks with additional methods/properties: computeVertexNormals on MockBufferGeometry, DoubleSide/FrontSide constants, camera.add method on MockPerspectiveCamera. Follow the exact same mock class patterns.
Reuse for all new meshManager tests to create MeshData objects with optional vertices, indices, normals parameters.

reuse.what: setup() function pattern in each test file
reuse.how: Follow the existing setup() function pattern that creates mocks and returns an object with all test dependencies. Extend as needed.
Extend SceneContext interface with adjustClipping method. Add to the return object from createScene, following the same pattern as resize().
Extend with refreshSelected() method following the same interface pattern. Add to return object from createSelection.
Add refreshSelected() and adjustClipping() calls within the existing mesh sync effect, after meshManager.sync(). This is the natural coordination point between mesh updates and dependent systems.
